revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:八王子権現]] {{Infobox deity|member_of={{ill|Sanjūbanshin|ja|三十番神|1=Thirty Guardian Deities|2=ja|3=三十番神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanjūbanshin|qq=}}|associated_deities ={{ill|Amatsuhikone|qid=Q10940228|1=Amatsuhikone}}; {{ill|Ame no Hohi|qid=Q10940685|1=Ame no Hohi}}; {{ill|Ichikishimahime|qid=Q11059454|1=Ichikishima-hime}}; {{ill|Ikutsuhikone|qid=Q11147583|1=Ikutsuhikone}}; {{ill|Kumanokusubi|qid=Q11298796|1=Kumanokusubi}}; {{ill|Oshihomimi|qid=Q462555|1=Ame-no-oshihomimi}}; {{ill|Tagitsuhime|qid=Q10932592|1=Tagitsuhime}}; {{ill|Takiribime|qid=Q10932870|1=Tagorihime}}|equivalent5_type=Japanese |equivalent5={{ill|Hasshōjin|qid=Q603995|1=Eight Directional Deities}}}}
{{afc comment |1=original page [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 19:57, 12 February 2025 (UTC)}}
'''Hachiōji Gongen''' (八王子権現) is a deity from the syncretism of mountain worship on Ushio Mountain (Hachiōji Mountain) in the former {{ill|Ōmi Province|en|Ōmi Province|qid=Q843882|lt=Ōmi Province|1=Ōmi Province}}, the {{ill|Tiantai|en|Tiantai|qid=Q668188|lt=Tendai|1=Tiantai}} school of Buddhism, and {{ill|Sannō Ichijitsu Shintō|en|Sannō Ichijitsu Shintō|qid=Q4407806|lt=Sannō Shintō|1=Sannō Ichijitsu Shintō}}. Hachiōji Gongen represents the eight {{ill|mikogami|ja|御子神|lt=child deities|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/mikogami|qq=}} (御子神, mikogami) of {{ill|Sannō Gongen|ja|山王権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sannō Gongen|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} or {{ill|Gozu Tennō|en|Gozu Tennō|qid=Q11570247|lt=Gozu Tennō|1=Gozu Tennō}}, who were venerated at shrines called '''Hachiōji-sha''' (八王子社) throughout Japan before the {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|en|Shinbutsu bunri|qid=Q1970535|lt=Shinbutsu bunri|1=Shinbutsu bunri}} and {{ill|Haibutsu kishaku|en|Haibutsu kishaku|qid=Q702298|lt=Haibutsu kishaku|1=Haibutsu kishaku}} movements. The deity is also associated with one of the {{ill|Sanjūbanshin|ja|三十番神|1=Thirty Guardian Deities|2=ja|3=三十番神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanjūbanshin|qq=}}, or thirty guardian deities of Japan.
The group are also known as the Yahashira-no-mikoto (八柱神)
== Yahashira-no-mikoto == In Japanese mythology, Susanoo-no-Mikoto, the storm god, is traditionally considered to have eight children, collectively known as the Yahashira-no-mikoto. These deities are enshrined alongside [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo]] and his wife, [[Kushinadahime]], at [[Yasaka Shrine]] in Kyoto.
The eight children are:
# '''[[Yashimajinumi]]-no-Kami (八島士奴美神)''': Also known as Ōnamuchi-no-Kami (大己貴神) in some texts, he is considered a central deity in the Izumo region. # '''[[Isotakeru-no-Mikoto]] (五十猛命)''': A deity associated with trees and forestry. # '''[[Oyatsuhime and Tsumatsuhime|Ōyatsuhime-no-Mikoto]] ([[:ja:大屋津姫命|大屋津姫命]])''': A goddess whose specific domains are less clearly defined in surviving texts. # '''[[Oyatsuhime and Tsumatsuhime|Tsumatsuhime-no-Mikoto]] (枛津姫命)''': Similar to Ōyatsuhime, details about her specific attributes are scarce. # '''[[Toshigami|Ōtoshi-no-Kami]] (大年神)''': A deity associated with the harvest and agriculture. # '''[[Ukanomitama|Ukanomitama-no-Kami]] (宇迦之御魂神)''': Often associated with food and grain, and sometimes identified with the deity Inari. # '''[[Oyabiko|Oyabiko-no-Kami]] ([[:ja:大屋毘古神|大屋毘古神]])''': A deity about whom little is detailed in the primary texts. # '''[[Suseri-Hime|Suseribime-no-Mikoto]] (須勢理毘売命)''': Also known as Wakasuserihime-no-Mikoto, she is noted in the Izumo Fudoki and is associated with the land of Izumo.
These deities play various roles within the Shinto pantheon, though detailed information about some of them is limited in historical texts.
The {{ill|Three Munakata Goddesses|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|lt=Three Munakata Goddesses|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Munakata Goddesses|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=}} are all daughters of Susanoo but they are traditionally excluded from this group.
== Content from [[Hachioji Shrine]] originally from [[:ja:八王子神社|八王子神社]] == Hachioji Shrines originated from the syncretic belief in {{ill|Hachioji Gongen|ja|八王子権現|1=Hachiōji Gongen|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachioji Gongen|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiōji Gongen|qq=}} under the system of {{ill|Shinbutsu-shūgō|en|Shinbutsu-shūgō|qid=Q2279088|lt=Shinbutsu-shūgō|1=Shinbutsu-shūgō}}.
In {{ill|913|en|913|qid=Q24860|lt=913|1=913}}, Buddhist monk Kegon Bosatsu Myōgyō had a vision of Gozu Tennō and eight princes while practicing asceticism at Fukazawa Mountain, currently {{ill|Shiroyama (Hachioji City)|ja|城山 (八王子市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiroyama (Hachioji City)|1=Mount Shiroyama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11426084}} in {{ill|Hachiōji|en|Hachiōji|qid=Q208863|lt=Hachioji City|1=Hachiōji}}, Tokyo. Inspired by this revelation, he enshrined {{ill|Hachioji Gongen|ja|八王子権現|1=Hachiōji Gongen|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachioji Gongen|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiōji Gongen|qq=}} at Fukazawa Mountain in {{ill|916|en|916|qid=Q24886|lt=916|1=916}}.
Gozu Tennō, regarded as a Buddhist guardian deity, is believed to have had eight children with {{ill|Hariti|ja|頗梨采女|1=Harisai-sho|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hariti|13=User:Immanuelle/Harisai-sho|qq=}}. These children are enshrined as the Hachioji deities.
The eight princes were later associated with eight directions and calendar deities, also known as {{ill|Hasshōshin|ja|八将神|1=Eight Directional Deities|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hasshōshin|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Directional Deities|qq=}}:
* {{ill|Taisai-shin|ja|太歳神|1=Taisaijin|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taisai-shin|13=User:Immanuelle/Taisaijin|qq=}} (Jupiter, Sōkō Tennō) * {{ill|Daishōgun|ja|大将軍 (方位神)|1=Daishōgun (directional deity)|lt=Daishōgun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daishōgun|13=User:Immanuelle/Daishōgun (directional deity)|qq=}} (Venus, Maō Tennō) * {{ill|Taion-shin|ja|太陰神|1=Taionjin|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taion-shin|13=User:Immanuelle/Taionjin|qq=}} (Saturn, Kumara Tennō) * {{ill|Saigyōshin|ja|歳刑神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saigyōshin|qq=}} (Mercury, Tokutatsu Tennō) * {{ill|Saiha Shin|ja|歳破神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saiha Shin|qq=}} (Saturn, Ryōji Tennō) * {{ill|Saisetsujin|ja|歳殺神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saisetsujin|qq=}} (Mars, Jishin Tennō) * {{ill|Ōbanjin|ja|黄幡神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōbanjin|qq=}} (Rahu, Takushin Tennō) * {{ill|Hyōbijin|ja|豹尾神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyōbijin|qq=}} (Ketu, Jakidokikami Tennō)
During the {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|en|Shinbutsu bunri|qid=Q1970535|lt=Shinbutsu bunri|1=Shinbutsu bunri}} of the Meiji era, the eight princes were redefined as the {{ill|Five Male and Three Female Deities|ja|五男三女神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Five Male and Three Female Deities|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to アマテラスとスサノオの誓約}}, born from the {{ill|Ukei|ja|誓約|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukei|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 宣誓|1=Oath|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q381045}} between {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|アマテラス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神}} and Susanoo:
* {{ill|Ame-no-Oshihomimi|ja|正勝吾勝勝速日天之忍穂耳命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Oshihomimi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to アメノオシホミミ|1=Ame-no-oshihomimi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q462555}} * {{ill|Ame-no-Hohi|ja|天之菩卑能命|1=Ame no Hohi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Hohi|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame no Hohi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to アメノホヒ}} * {{ill|Amatsuhikone|ja|天津日子根命|1=Amatsuhikone|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amatsuhikone|13=User:Immanuelle/Amatsuhikone|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to アマツヒコネ}} * {{ill|Ikutsuhikone|ja|活津日子根命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikutsuhikone|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イクツヒコネ|1=Ikutsuhikone|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11147583}} * {{ill|Kumanokusubi|ja|熊野久須毘命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumanokusubi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to クマノクスビ}} * {{ill|Takiribime|ja|多紀理毘売命|1=Tagorihime|2=ja|3=タキリビメ|4=fr|5=Takiribime|6=zh|7=多紀理毘賣命|1=Tagorihime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takiribime|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} * {{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|市寸島比売命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime||1=Ichikishima-hime|2=fr|3=Ichikishimahime|4=ja|5=イチキシマヒメ|6=zh|7=市寸島比賣命|8=ru|9=Итикишима-химэ|10=de|11=Ichikishima-hime}} * {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|多岐都比売命|1=Tagitsuhime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タギツヒメ}}
Certain shrines enshrine different deities. For example, at {{ill|Yasaka Shrine|en|Yasaka Shrine|qid=Q692714|lt=Yasaka Shrine|1=Yasaka Shrine}}, the eight princes are identified as [[Yashimajinumi]], {{ill|Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|ja|五十猛神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Isotakeru-no-kami|en:draft|10=fr|11=Isotakeru-no-kami|qid=Q10881556|lt=Isotakeru-no-kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|qq=|1=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto}}, and other deities.
{{ill|Daishōgun Hachi Shrine|ja|大将軍八神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daishōgun Hachi Shrine|qq=}} identifies the two as such
* {{ill|Taisaijin|ja|太歳神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taisaijin|qq=}} ({{ill|Ame-no-oshihomimi|en|Ame-no-oshihomimi|qid=Q462555|lt=Ame-no-oshihomimi|1=Ame-no-oshihomimi}}) * {{ill|Taionjin|ja|太陰神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taionjin|qq=}} ({{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|市杵嶋姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime||1=Ichikishima-hime|2=fr|3=Ichikishimahime|4=ja|5=イチキシマヒメ|6=zh|7=市寸島比賣命|8=ru|9=Итикишима-химэ|10=de|11=Ichikishima-hime}}) * {{ill|Saigyōshin|ja|歳刑神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saigyōshin|qq=}} ({{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心媛命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タキリビメ}}) * {{ill|Saiha Shin|ja|歳破神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saiha Shin|qq=}} ({{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|湍津姫命|1=Tagitsuhime|2=ja|3=タギツヒメ|4=zh|5=多岐都比賣命|6=fr|7=Tagitsuhime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|qq=}}) * {{ill|Saisetsujin|ja|歳殺神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saisetsujin|qq=}} ({{ill|Ame no Hohi|ja|天穂日神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame no Hohi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to アメノホヒ}}) * {{ill|Ōbanjin|ja|黄幡神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōbanjin|qq=}} ({{ill|Ikutsuhikone|ja|活津彦根神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikutsuhikone|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イクツヒコネ|1=Ikutsuhikone|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11147583}}) * {{ill|Hyōbijin|ja|豹尾神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyōbijin|qq=}} ({{ill|Kumanokusubi|ja|熊野櫲樟日命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumanokusubi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to クマノクスビ}})
There are two dramatically different groups {| class="wikitable" |+ !{{ill|Daishōgun Hachi Shrine|ja|大将軍八神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daishōgun Hachi Shrine|lt=Hachi Shrine Onmyodo}} !{{ill|Daishōgun Hachi Shrine|ja|大将軍八神社|qq=Hachi Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daishōgun Hachi Shrine|lt=Hachi Shrine Kami}} ![[Yasaka Shrine]] !Notes |- |Omitted |Omitted |'''[[Yashimajinumi]]''' | |- |Omitted |Omitted |'''[[Isotakeru-no-Mikoto]]''' | |- |Omitted |Omitted |'''[[Oyatsuhime and Tsumatsuhime|Ōyatsuhime-no-Mikoto]]''' | |- |Omitted |Omitted |'''[[Oyatsuhime and Tsumatsuhime|Tsumatsuhime-no-Mikoto]]''' | |- |Omitted |Omitted |'''[[Toshigami|Ōtoshi-no-Kami]]''' | |- |Omitted |Omitted |'''[[Ukanomitama|Ukanomitama-no-Kami]]''' | |- |Omitted |Omitted |'''[[Oyabiko|Oyabiko-no-Kami]]''' | |- |Omitted |Omitted |'''[[Suseri-Hime|Suseribime-no-Mikoto]]''' | |- ! colspan="4" |{{ill|Daishōgun Hachi Shrine|ja|大将軍八神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daishōgun Hachi Shrine}} |- !{{ill|Daishōgun Hachi Shrine|ja|大将軍八神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daishōgun Hachi Shrine|lt=Hachi Shrine Onmyodo}} !{{ill|Daishōgun Hachi Shrine|ja|大将軍八神社|qq=Hachi Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daishōgun Hachi Shrine|lt=Hachi Shrine Kami}} ![[Yasaka Shrine]] !Notes |- |{{ill|Daishōgun (directional deity)|ja|大将軍 (方位神)|lt=Daishōgun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daishōgun (directional deity)}} |[[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] | | |- |{{ill|Taisaijin|ja|太歳神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taisaijin}} |{{ill|Ame-no-oshihomimi|en|Ame-no-oshihomimi|qid=Q462555|lt=Ame-no-oshihomimi|1=Ame-no-oshihomimi}} | |[[Amaterasu]] 1st son |- |{{ill|Taionjin|ja|太陰神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taionjin}} |{{ill|Ichikishima-hime|fr|Ichikishimahime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|ja|5=イチキシマヒメ|6=zh|7=市寸島比賣命|8=ru|9=Итикишима-химэ|10=de|11=Ichikishima-hime}} | |[[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo]] 3rd daughter |- |{{ill|Saigyōshin|ja|歳刑神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saigyōshin}} |{{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心媛命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タキリビメ}} | |[[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo]] 1st daughter |- |{{ill|Saiha Shin|ja|歳破神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saiha Shin}} |{{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|タギツヒメ|4=zh|5=多岐都比賣命|6=fr|7=Tagitsuhime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime}} | |[[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo]] 2nd daughter |- |{{ill|Saisetsujin|ja|歳殺神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saisetsujin}} |{{ill|Ame no Hohi|ja|天穂日神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame no Hohi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to アメノホヒ}} | |[[Amaterasu]] 2nd son |- |{{ill|Ōbanjin|ja|黄幡神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōbanjin}} |{{ill|Ikutsuhikone|en|Ikutsuhikone|qid=Q11147583|lt=Ikutsuhikone|1=Ikutsuhikone}} | |[[Amaterasu]] 4th son |- |{{ill|Hyōbijin|ja|豹尾神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyōbijin}} |{{ill|Kumanokusubi|ja|熊野櫲樟日命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumanokusubi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to クマノクスビ}} | |[[Amaterasu]] 5th son |- |Omitted |{{ill|Amatsuhikone|ja|天津日子根命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amatsuhikone|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to アマツヒコネ}} | |[[Amaterasu]] 3rd son |} It omits {{ill|Amatsuhikone|ja|天津日子根命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amatsuhikone|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to アマツヒコネ}} as Susanoo is in the group
== Origins == Ushio Mountain, a peak of {{ill|Otokoyama (Shiga and Kyoto)|ja|音羽山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otokoyama (Shiga and Kyoto)|1=otowayama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11664333}}, was historically known as Ushio (主穂) Mountain, symbolizing offerings of the first harvest to deities as the mountain of the household’s protector. The summit’s {{ill|Ushio Shrine|ja|牛尾神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ushio Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, historically called Ushio-no-miya, was one of the 21 auxiliary shrines of {{ill|Hiyoshi Taisha|en|Hiyoshi Taisha|qid=Q656451|lt=Hiyoshi Taisha|1=Hiyoshi Taisha}}, which served as the {{ill|Chinjugami|en|Chinjugami|qid=Q97479976|lt=Chinjugami|1=Chinjugami}} of {{ill|Enryaku-ji|en|Enryaku-ji|qid=Q917195|lt=Enryaku-ji|1=Enryaku-ji}} on {{ill|Mount Hiei|en|Mount Hiei|qid=Q748040|lt=Mount Hiei|1=Mount Hiei}}. The eight child deities of Sannō, known as Hachiōji Gongen, were enshrined there with {{ill|Senju Kannon|ja|千手観音|1=Thousand-armed Kannon|2=ja|3=千手観音|lt=Thousand-Armed Kannon|4=fr|5=Thousand-armed Kannon|6=ko|7=관세음보살|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senju Kannon|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} as their honji (本地仏, Buddhist counterpart).
Additionally, Hachiōji Gongen is connected to {{ill|Gozu Tennō|en|Gozu Tennō|qid=Q11570247|lt=Gozu Tennō|1=Gozu Tennō}}, the protective deity of {{ill|Jetavana|en|Jetavana|qid=Q305242|lt=Jetavana monastery|1=Jetavana}}, and his consort Harisainyo (頗梨采女), believed to be the parents of the eight child deities. Over time, these beliefs merged into the syncretic worship of Hachiōji Gongen.
<gallery> File:Hiyoshi Taisha - at the summit.jpg|Ushio Shrine (former Ushio-no-miya) </gallery>
== Overview == The eight child deities of Sannō Gongen or Gozu Tennō—namely Sōkō, Maō, Kuma, Tokutatsu, Ryōji, Tanikan, Jishinsō, and Takusō—were believed to govern the eight cardinal directions. Each deity was associated with a Buddhist counterpart, such as Shakyamuni Tathāgata, Manjushri, and Amitabha, as recorded in the {{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|国史大辞典|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokushi Daijiten|1=National History Encyclopedia|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q6426904}}.<ref name="kokushi">''Kokushi Daijiten'', "Hachiōji" entry, p. 586.</ref> The cult of these deities, influenced by {{ill|Onmyōdō|en|Onmyōdō|qid=Q1340540|lt=Onmyōdō|1=Onmyōdō}} and boundary-deity worship, promised protection from diseases and disasters.
The {{ill|The Tale of the Heike|en|The Tale of the Heike|qid=Q830740|lt=Heike Monogatari|1=The Tale of the Heike}} recounts a story in which the regent {{ill|Fujiwara no Moromichi|en|Fujiwara no Moromichi|qid=Q923640|lt=Fujiwara no Moromichi|1=Fujiwara no Moromichi}} was struck down by a divine arrow of Hachiōji Gongen following a curse by the monks of Mount Hiei.
== Decline and Transition == The {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|en|Shinbutsu bunri|qid=Q1970535|lt=Shinbutsu bunri|1=Shinbutsu bunri}} and {{ill|Haibutsu kishaku|en|Haibutsu kishaku|qid=Q702298|lt=Haibutsu kishaku|1=Haibutsu kishaku}} policies during the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|en|Meiji Restoration|qid=Q8707|lt=Meiji Restoration|1=Meiji Restoration}} led to the dissolution of Hachiōji Gongen worship alongside the disbandment of Sannō Gongen and Gozu Tennō cults. Many Hachiōji shrines were renamed, adopting titles such as {{ill|Hiyoshi Hachiōji Shrine|ja|日吉八王子神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiyoshi Hachiōji Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135504563|1=Hiyoshi Hachiōji Shrine}}, Hachiōji Shrine, or Ushio Shrine.
== See Also == * {{ill|Hasshōjin|ja|八将神|1=Eight Directional Deities|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hasshōjin|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Directional Deities|qq=}} * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|en|Ōyamatsumi|qid=Q386563|qid=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi|1=Ōyamatsumi}} * {{ill|Sannō Ichijitsu Shintō|en|Sannō Ichijitsu Shintō|qid=Q4407806|lt=Sannō Shintō|1=Sannō Ichijitsu Shintō}} * {{ill|Hokke Shintō|ja|法華神道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hokke Shintō|1=Hokke Shintō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11555328}} * {{ill|Hachiōji (disambiguation)|ja|八王子 (曖昧さ回避)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiōji (disambiguation)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 八王子}} * {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞嗚神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=}}
